steps.title: Contrast the orbital periods
steps.content: The goal is to contrast Iapetus and Mimas. The notes give a clear difference: Iapetus takes 79.33 Earth days on average to orbit Saturn, while Mimas takes only 0.94 Earth days.

steps.title: Reject unsupported similarities
steps.content: Choice A invents a circular orbit for Mimas, which the notes do not support. Choices B and D focus on what the moons share through Kepler's laws, not on a contrast. The remaining choice states the speed difference supported by the orbital periods. The answer is C.
